mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression or quarterly returns are negative when both series expand.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated industrial production levels reflect strong manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, generating positive short-term market momentum over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO levels (t-stat < 1.5) or direction flips negative during normalization phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 230 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures, prompting expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and yields negative monthly returns.
failure_condition: Market prices in rate hikes efficiently, resulting in positive or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment confirms labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending capacity and corporate profit margins for positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict quarterly returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or unemployment rises concurrently with payrolls without negative market impact.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP levels reflect robust aggregate economic activity, reinforcing positive investor sentiment and generating upward price pressure in equities over the immediate five-day window.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero predictive power for 5-day returns (R-squared < 0.01) or returns are negative during high-GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: A manufacturing output growth rate falling below 0.5 percent month-over-month signals supply-side stagnation, reducing corporate earnings expectations and triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when the growth threshold is breached.
